profile.name: Putra Christian
profile.headline: Creative Front-End Developer building playful, polished, people-friendly web experiences
profile.shortBio: Front-End Developer with 5+ years of experience building responsive websites, interactive interfaces, and digital experiences that mix personality with clarity.
profile.longBio: I like frontend that feels alive. Sometimes that means bold layout, sometimes it means tiny interaction details, and sometimes it simply means making something clearer, faster, and more enjoyable to use. My professional background includes years of shipping work in a fast-moving environment at Garena, but I do not want to be boxed into one niche. What I bring is broader: creative UI thinking, responsive implementation, interface polish, and the ability to turn visual ideas into real web experiences people want to explore.
profile.availability: Open to freelance, contract, and creative collaborations.
profile.avatarAlt: Animated avatar video for Putra Christian

hero.headlineSuffix: for a frontend developer who likes lively interfaces, visual rhythm, and web experiences that people actually enjoy using.
hero.subheadline: This portfolio is rebuilt like a growing collection instead of a static brochure. It is designed to stay fun, flexible, and easy to update whenever a new project gets added.

experience.title: Associate Front End Web Developer
experience.summary: Builds interactive web experiences, frontend features, and polished UI in a fast-moving production environment.
highlights: Creates interfaces with React.js, Redux, and JavaScript (ES6+) across responsive web experiences.
Collaborates with designers, backend developers, and product stakeholders to turn concepts into production-ready UI.
Optimizes performance and loading speed so rich interfaces stay smooth and dependable.
Works with clean code, Git workflows, and cross-device implementation best practices.

experience.title: Internship Front End Web Developer
experience.summary: Built early production experience through testing, debugging, and UI implementation support.
highlights: Helped test and debug shipped web experiences.
Assisted with UI component implementation and static page development using HTML and CSS.

speaking.title: Create Your Own Personal Page with React JS
speaking.organization: AMBIZ x Himpunan Mahasiswa Informatika ITB
speaking.summary: Delivered a public webinar about building a personal page with React JS.
speaking.posterAlt: Poster for the Create Your Own Personal Page with React JS webinar.

speaking.title: Belajar bikin game bareng Garena
speaking.summary: Shared frontend web development principles in a collaborative workshop setting.
speaking.posterAlt: Poster for the Belajar bikin game bareng Garena webinar.

speaking.title: How to Develop a Web Game
speaking.summary: Presented practical frontend thinking and best practices for interactive web-based experiences.
speaking.posterAlt: Poster for the How to Develop a Web Game talk.

process.title: Collect ideas like references on a moodboard
process.description: I usually start by understanding the feeling, energy, and visual direction the interface should carry before worrying about details.

process.title: Make it fun but still obvious
process.description: I like interfaces with personality, but only when they still feel easy to navigate and easy to understand.

process.title: Turn the nice idea into solid frontend
process.description: The build still needs to be responsive, maintainable, and smooth enough to survive real use, not just look good in a screenshot.

chatPrompts: What kind of frontend work does Putra enjoy most?
Can his experience fit projects outside gaming?
What technologies does he use most often?
How would you describe his frontend style?

faqs.question: Why are there no live demo links for every project?
faqs.answer: Some of the work comes from private or time-limited commercial projects, so this portfolio often uses screenshots and public-safe summaries instead of full live demos.

faqs.question: Is he only suitable for gaming projects?
faqs.answer: No. The portfolio is intentionally positioned more broadly. The same frontend strengths can fit product sites, branded campaigns, landing pages, interactive websites, and other digital experiences.

faqs.question: Why is the site built like a project library?
faqs.answer: Because the goal is long-term growth. New projects can be added continuously without redesigning the whole portfolio each time.

projects.title: Interactive Launch Experience
projects.subtitle: Bold layout, layered storytelling, and responsive interaction in one screen-driven experience.
projects.role: Frontend implementation, responsive UI, visual polish
projects.summary: A project type built around first impressions, strong composition, and smooth interaction. It is less about one niche and more about translating visual intent into something engaging on the web.
contributions: Implemented the frontend UI and interaction patterns for a visually driven experience.
Balanced expressive design with readable content structure and strong action hierarchy.
Built a flexible layout that could adapt to content changes without losing rhythm.
outcomes: A reusable structure for future screenshot-led case studies.

projects.title: Content-Rich Microsite
projects.subtitle: A roomy interface built around atmosphere, pacing, and content balance.
projects.role: Frontend development, modular section build, cross-device adaptation
projects.summary: This kind of project shows how to shape a lot of content into something still fun to browse, with large visuals, modular sections, and plenty of responsive decisions.
contributions: Translated visual direction into modular frontend sections.
Maintained a strong balance between mood, readability, and structure.
Adapted image-heavy layouts for smaller screens without flattening the experience.
outcomes: A more expressive interface that still feels easy to follow.
Section patterns that support future content reuse.
